title: Senior Aerospace Structural Engineer
description: AeroVista Dynamics is seeking a Senior Aerospace Structural Engineer to lead structural analysis and design of launch vehicle components. You will perform FEA using ANSYS Mechanical for static, dynamic, and buckling load cases on primary and secondary structures. CATIA V5 is our primary CAD tool for detailed part and assembly modeling. The role requires deep expertise in GD&T per ASME Y14.5, fatigue analysis, and composite materials. You will generate stress reports per AS9100 documentation requirements and mentor junior stress engineers. Systems engineering knowledge is critical for interface load management across vehicle subsystems.
requirements:
- BS in Aerospace or Mechanical Engineering (MS preferred)
- 8+ years structural analysis experience
- ANSYS Mechanical expert
- CATIA V5 for CAD modeling
- FEA for static, dynamic, and buckling analysis
- GD&T per ASME Y14.5
- Fatigue analysis and damage tolerance
- Composite structures (CFRP, honeycomb)
- AS9100 documentation standards
- Systems engineering for interface loads

title: UAV Design Engineer
description: NovaDrone Technologies designs next-generation autonomous drones for commercial inspection and delivery. We are hiring a UAV Design Engineer to develop airframe structures, mechanism designs, and payload integration solutions. SolidWorks is our primary CAD platform. You will perform structural FEA using ANSYS, CFD for aerodynamic optimization, and lightweight optimization to maximize flight endurance. The role involves GD&T documentation, DFM review for CNC and composite manufacturing, and close collaboration with our flight software team. Experience with ROS2 for hardware-software integration is a strong plus.
requirements:
- BS in Aerospace or Mechanical Engineering
- 3-5 years UAV or small aircraft design
- SolidWorks proficiency required
- FEA using ANSYS for structural validation
- CFD for aerodynamic analysis
- Lightweight optimization (topology/generative design)
- Composites (carbon fiber layup design)
- GD&T per ASME Y14.5
- DFM for CNC and composite manufacturing
- ROS2 for hardware-software integration preferred

title: Propulsion Engineer
description: Skyward Propulsion develops liquid and solid rocket propulsion systems for commercial launch vehicles. We need a Propulsion Engineer to design turbopump components, perform thermal analysis of combustion chambers, and validate designs through FEA and testing. CATIA V6 is used for CAD modeling, and ANSYS Fluent for CFD of internal flow paths. You will work with materials engineers on high-temperature alloy selection and document designs per AS9100. GD&T expertise is required for tight-tolerance rotating components. This is a fast-paced NewSpace environment where you will own designs from concept through hot-fire testing.
requirements:
- BS in Aerospace or Mechanical Engineering (MS preferred)
- 3+ years propulsion or turbomachinery experience
- CATIA V5/V6 for component modeling
- ANSYS Fluent for CFD analysis
- FEA for thermal and structural analysis
- GD&T for rotating components
- High-temperature materials knowledge
- AS9100 documentation
- Thermal analysis of combustion systems
- Test planning and data analysis

title: Robotics Systems Engineer
description: Atlas Robotics builds collaborative robots for manufacturing and logistics applications. We are seeking a Robotics Systems Engineer to design mechanical systems, integrate sensors, and develop control algorithms. SolidWorks is used for robot arm design, and FEA in ANSYS validates structural integrity under dynamic loading. You will program robot behaviors in ROS2 using Python and C++, implement path planning algorithms, and test in simulation before hardware deployment. The role requires systems engineering thinking to manage mechanical, electrical, and software interfaces. GD&T documentation ensures manufacturing precision.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical or Robotics Engineering (MS preferred)
- 3+ years robotics systems experience
- SolidWorks for mechanical design
- FEA in ANSYS for dynamic analysis
- ROS2 programming (Python and C++)
- Path planning and motion control
- Systems engineering for multi-domain integration
- GD&T per ASME Y14.5
- Sensor integration (force/torque, vision)
- Simulation and testing methodology

title: Thermal Engineer - Spacecraft
description: Helios Space Systems designs thermal control systems for satellites and deep-space probes. We need a Thermal Engineer to develop thermal models, perform thermal analysis using ANSYS and MATLAB, and design passive and active thermal control hardware. You will model orbital thermal environments, predict component temperatures during mission phases, and specify heaters, thermostats, and MLI blankets. CATIA is used for thermal hardware CAD modeling. The role requires AS9100 documentation, GD&T for thermal interface specifications, and systems engineering coordination with other subsystem teams.
requirements:
- BS in Aerospace or Mechanical Engineering (MS preferred)
- 3+ years spacecraft thermal engineering
- Thermal analysis using ANSYS and/or Thermal Desktop
- MATLAB for thermal modeling and simulation
- CATIA for thermal hardware design
- Passive and active thermal control design
- Orbital thermal environment modeling
- GD&T for thermal interfaces
- AS9100 documentation
- Systems engineering for subsystem coordination

title: Stress Engineer
description: Apex Aerostructures manufactures wing and fuselage components for commercial aircraft. We are hiring a Stress Engineer to perform structural analysis of metallic and composite aerostructures. You will use ANSYS and Nastran for FEA, calculate margins of safety, and generate stress reports per aerospace standards. CATIA V5 is the primary CAD tool. The role requires expertise in fatigue analysis, damage tolerance, and composite failure criteria (Tsai-Wu, Hashin). GD&T interpretation is essential for understanding part manufacturing constraints. Technical writing skills are needed for producing formal stress reports.
requirements:
- BS in Aerospace or Mechanical Engineering
- 4+ years stress analysis experience
- ANSYS and/or MSC Nastran for FEA
- CATIA V5 for geometry interpretation
- Fatigue and damage tolerance analysis
- Composite failure analysis (Tsai-Wu, Hashin)
- GD&T per ASME Y14.5
- AS9100 stress report documentation
- Structural analysis hand calculations
- Technical writing for formal reports

title: Flight Systems Mechanical Engineer
description: Orion Defense Systems develops advanced flight systems for defense applications. We need a Flight Systems Mechanical Engineer to design mechanical assemblies for guidance, navigation, and control hardware. SolidWorks and CATIA are used for CAD modeling. You will perform FEA for vibration and shock qualification using ANSYS, design for extreme environments (-40C to +85C), and manage tolerance stack-ups across complex assemblies. GD&T documentation per ASME Y14.5 is mandatory. The role requires systems engineering for managing interfaces between mechanical, electrical, and software subsystems.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical or Aerospace Engineering
- 5+ years flight hardware mechanical design
- SolidWorks and/or CATIA
- ANSYS FEA for vibration and shock
- Thermal analysis for extreme environments
- GD&T per ASME Y14.5
- Tolerance stack-up analysis
- Systems engineering for interface management
- AS9100 compliance
- US Citizenship required (defense projects)

title: Robotics Software Engineer
description: TerraBot Automation develops mobile robots for warehouse automation. We need a Robotics Software Engineer to implement navigation, perception, and manipulation algorithms. You will write C++ and Python code within the ROS2 framework, develop SLAM algorithms, and integrate LIDAR and camera sensors. The role bridges software and mechanical engineering — you will work closely with the mechanical design team (SolidWorks) to ensure sensor mounting and cable routing are robust. Understanding of structural analysis for robot chassis design and systems engineering for full-stack integration is valuable.

- BS in Robotics, Mechanical, or Computer Engineering (MS preferred)
- 3+ years robotics software development
- C++ and Python proficiency
- ROS2 framework experience
- SLAM and path planning algorithms
- Sensor integration (LIDAR, cameras, IMU)
- SolidWorks familiarity for mechanical interface
- Systems engineering mindset
- Simulation tools (Gazebo, Isaac Sim)
- Strong debugging and testing skills

title: Composite Structures Engineer
description: AeroCraft Composites specializes in advanced composite structures for aerospace applications. We are hiring a Composite Structures Engineer to design carbon fiber and fiberglass structures, develop layup schedules, and perform structural analysis. SolidWorks and CATIA are used for ply modeling and tool design. FEA in ANSYS validates structural performance under flight loads. You will conduct fatigue analysis, apply composite failure criteria, and document designs per AS9100. GD&T for composite part specifications requires understanding of manufacturing variability. - Fatigue analysis for composite structures
- GD&T for composite parts
- AS9100 documentation
- DFM for autoclave and OOA processes
- Laminate analysis (classical lamination theory)

title: Mechanisms Engineer

- GD&T per ASME Y14.5

- AS9100 documentation

title: Senior Robotics Engineer
description: The Autonomous Systems Lab develops next-generation robotic platforms for healthcare and logistics. We need a Senior Robotics Engineer to lead mechanical design of robot platforms, integrate sensors and actuators, and coordinate with software teams on ROS2-based control systems. SolidWorks is used for all mechanical design. FEA in ANSYS validates structural and dynamic performance. You will apply lightweight optimization, specify motors and transmissions, and mentor junior engineers. C++ and Python are used for hardware interface code. Systems engineering manages the full robot architecture. GD&T ensures manufacturing precision for precision mechanism components.
requirements:
- BS/MS in Mechanical or Robotics Engineering
- 7+ years robotics mechanical design
- SolidWorks expert proficiency
- ANSYS FEA for dynamic analysis
- ROS2 integration (C++ and Python)
- Lightweight optimization for payload capacity
- Motor and transmission specification
- GD&T per ASME Y14.5
- Systems engineering for robot architecture
- Technical leadership and mentoring

title: Spacecraft Structures Analyst
description: GravityWell Space develops reusable launch vehicles. We need a Spacecraft Structures Analyst to perform detailed FEA of tank structures, interstage adapters, and payload fairings using ANSYS. CATIA V5 models define the geometry. You will calculate margins of safety, perform buckling analysis, and assess fatigue life for reusable structures. Composite and metallic material analysis is required. GD&T documentation per ASME Y14.5 supports manufacturing. Lightweight optimization is critical for vehicle performance. Python and MATLAB automate analysis workflows. Systems engineering coordinates structural analysis with propulsion and thermal teams. AS9100 compliance is required.
requirements:
- BS in Aerospace or Mechanical Engineering (MS preferred)
- 4+ years structural analysis for launch vehicles
- ANSYS FEA expert (static, buckling, dynamic)
- CATIA V5 for geometry interpretation
- Composite and metallic analysis
- Fatigue analysis for reusable structures
- GD&T per ASME Y14.5
- Lightweight optimization
- Python and MATLAB for automation
- Systems engineering and AS9100

title: Robotics Controls Engineer
description: MotionIQ Robotics develops high-speed pick-and-place robots for electronics manufacturing. We need a Robotics Controls Engineer to design and tune servo control systems, implement trajectory planning algorithms, and optimize robot dynamic performance. C++ and Python are used within the ROS2 framework. You will work with mechanical designers (SolidWorks) on arm dynamics, perform vibration analysis to minimize resonance, and use MATLAB/Simulink for control system simulation. Understanding of structural FEA for validating robot arm rigidity and systems engineering for the full control stack is important.
requirements:
- BS/MS in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering
- 3+ years servo control and motion planning
- C++ and Python proficiency
- ROS2 framework experience
- MATLAB/Simulink for control simulation
- SolidWorks familiarity for mechanical interface
- Vibration analysis for dynamic tuning
- FEA awareness for arm rigidity
- Systems engineering for controls architecture
- High-speed trajectory planning experience

title: Aerospace Quality Engineer
description: QualityAero Solutions provides quality engineering services for aerospace manufacturers. We need an Aerospace Quality Engineer to manage AS9100 compliance, conduct supplier audits, and lead root cause analysis on non-conforming hardware. GD&T expertise is critical for interpreting drawings and verifying machined and fabricated parts. You will facilitate DFMEA reviews, manage SPC programs for critical characteristics, and generate customer quality reports. SolidWorks drawing review capability ensures accurate disposition of non-conformances. Understanding of CNC machining, composite fabrication, and additive manufacturing quality features of our business is important. ISO 9001 is the foundation of our quality system.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical or Aerospace Engineering
- 4+ years aerospace quality engineering
- AS9100 expert (lead auditor preferred)
- GD&T expert per ASME Y14.5
- DFMEA facilitation
- SPC for critical characteristics
- Root cause analysis (8D methodology)
- SolidWorks drawing review
- CNC and composite quality knowledge
- ISO 9001 foundation and technical writing

title: Rocket Engine Test Engineer
description: FlamePoint Propulsion develops and tests liquid rocket engines. We need a Rocket Engine Test Engineer to design test stands, instrument engines, and analyze hot-fire test data. SolidWorks is used for test stand structural design with GD&T documentation. FEA in ANSYS validates structures under thrust and thermal loads. MATLAB and Python process high-speed pressure and temperature data. The role requires thermal analysis expertise, understanding of propulsion systems, and AS9100 test documentation. Systems engineering coordinates test objectives with the design team. Technical writing produces formal test reports.
requirements:
- BS in Aerospace or Mechanical Engineering
- 3+ years propulsion or test engineering
- SolidWorks for test stand design
- ANSYS FEA for structural and thermal analysis
- MATLAB and Python for data processing
- GD&T per ASME Y14.5
- Thermal analysis for combustion systems
- AS9100 test documentation
- Systems engineering coordination
- Technical writing for test reports

title: EOAT Design Engineer - Robotics
description: GripStar Solutions designs end-of-arm tooling for industrial robots in automotive and aerospace manufacturing. We need an EOAT Design Engineer to create custom gripper and fixture designs in SolidWorks. You will specify pneumatic actuators, vacuum cups, and mechanical clamps. FEA validates tooling under dynamic loads from robot motion. GD&T ensures tooling meets part positioning requirements. DFM review for CNC and 3D printing drives efficient fabrication. You will integrate tooling with FANUC and ABB robots and PLC cell controllers. Lightweight optimization maximizes robot payload capacity.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical Engineering
- 3+ years EOAT or fixture design
- SolidWorks proficiency
- FEA for dynamic load validation
- Pneumatic actuator specification
- GD&T per ASME Y14.5
- DFM for CNC and 3D printing
- 3D printing for prototyping
- FANUC/ABB robot integration
- Lightweight optimization for payload

title: CFD Analyst - Propulsion
description: ThrustDynamics Aerospace performs CFD analysis for rocket engine and gas turbine clients. We need a CFD Analyst to run complex internal and external flow simulations using ANSYS Fluent. You will model combustion, heat transfer, and multi-phase flows. CATIA is used for geometry preparation. MATLAB and Python automate meshing and post-processing workflows. The role requires strong thermal analysis skills, understanding of structural loads from pressure fields, and technical writing for analysis reports. AS9100 and systems engineering discipline govern our work.
requirements:
- BS in Aerospace or Mechanical Engineering (MS preferred)
- 3+ years CFD analysis experience
- ANSYS Fluent expert
- CATIA for geometry preparation
- Thermal analysis of propulsion systems
- MATLAB and Python for automation
- CFD meshing best practices
- AS9100 analysis documentation
- Systems engineering for load coordination
- Technical writing for analysis reports

title: Satellite Integration Technician Lead
description: MicroSat Assemblies integrates CubeSat and SmallSat platforms. We need a Satellite Integration Technician Lead to manage clean room assembly, perform mechanical integration of subsystems, and verify fit per GD&T specifications. SolidWorks assembly models guide integration procedures. You will torque fasteners per specifications, perform harnessing, and support vibration and thermal vacuum testing. AS9100 work instructions govern all activities. Understanding of composite and metallic structures, systems engineering integration sequences, and tolerance stack-up verification is required. Technical writing produces integration procedures and as-built documentation.

- 4+ years spacecraft integration experience
- SolidWorks for assembly model review
- GD&T per ASME Y14.5
- Clean room assembly procedures
- AS9100 work instruction compliance
- Vibration and TVAC test support
- Composite and metallic hardware handling
- Tolerance stack-up verification
- Technical writing for integration procedures

title: Helicopter Rotor Dynamics Engineer
description: RotorTech Aerospace designs rotor systems for military and commercial helicopters. We need a Rotor Dynamics Engineer to perform vibration analysis of rotating components, analyze blade fatigue life, and optimize rotor hub designs. CATIA V5 is used for CAD modeling, and ANSYS for structural and dynamic FEA. MATLAB analyzes frequency response data and rotor dynamics models. The role requires deep knowledge of fatigue analysis, composite blade structures, and GD&T for precision rotating components. Systems engineering coordinates rotor design with flight control requirements. AS9100 documentation is standard.

- ANSYS FEA for dynamic and fatigue analysis

title: Space Mechanisms Test Engineer
description: DeployTech Space designs deployment mechanisms for solar arrays and antennas. We need a Space Mechanisms Test Engineer to plan and execute functional, vibration, and thermal vacuum tests on flight mechanism hardware. SolidWorks designs test fixtures and ground support equipment. FEA in ANSYS predicts test environments. MATLAB and Python process high-speed camera and sensor data from deployment tests. GD&T inspection verifies mechanism tolerances before and after testing. Fatigue analysis informs test load levels. AS9100 governs test documentation. title: Autonomous Drone Software Engineer
description: SkySense Autonomy develops autonomous flight software for commercial drones. We need an Autonomous Drone Software Engineer to implement perception, planning, and control algorithms. You will write C++ and Python within the ROS2 framework, integrate LIDAR, cameras, and IMU sensors, and test in simulation (Gazebo, AirSim). While primarily a software role, understanding of drone mechanical design (SolidWorks models), structural analysis constraints, and systems engineering is essential for designing robust autonomous behaviors. title: Aerospace Materials Engineer
description: MateriAero Technologies is a materials testing and consulting firm serving aerospace OEMs. We need an Aerospace Materials Engineer to characterize metallic and composite material properties, conduct coupon testing, and generate material allowables for structural analysis. SolidWorks designs test specimens and fixtures. ANSYS FEA correlates with test data. The role requires understanding of fatigue properties, composite layup effects, and lightweight optimization through material selection. GD&T applies to specimen specifications. title: Spacecraft Dynamics Analyst
description: OrbitalMech Systems performs orbital mechanics and spacecraft dynamics analysis. We need a Spacecraft Dynamics Analyst to model rigid body and flexible body dynamics for satellites during deployment, docking, and attitude maneuvers. MATLAB and Python are the primary tools for dynamics modeling. ANSYS provides FEA mode shapes for flexible body integration. CATIA V5 provides mass properties from CAD models. The role requires systems engineering to coordinate dynamics requirements across subsystems, vibration analysis for spacecraft qualification, and technical writing for formal analysis reports. AS9100 documentation is standard. GD&T knowledge helps interpret mechanism tolerances affecting dynamics. Fatigue analysis may be needed for deployment mechanisms.
requirements:
- BS in Aerospace or Mechanical Engineering (MS preferred)
- 3+ years spacecraft dynamics or orbital mechanics
- MATLAB and Python for dynamics modeling
- ANSYS FEA for mode shapes
- CATIA V5 for mass properties
- Vibration analysis for qualification
- Systems engineering for dynamics integration
- Technical writing for analysis reports
- AS9100 documentation
- GD&T and fatigue analysis awareness

MATLAB prototypes signal processing algorithms. Systems engineering connects perception outputs to motion planning inputs. description: ThermoSat Systems designs thermal control hardware for satellite programs.
